topic: A person finds out their biography was written without their knowledge
raw_output:
[story 1]

The glossy paper felt cold beneath my fingertips as I unfolded the document. It was my life, laid out in meticulous, damning detail—dates, accomplishments, even the mundane details of my childhood that I’d only vaguely recalled. My breath hitched, a sharp, involuntary intake of air that seemed too loud in the quiet study. Every sentence was a precise placement, a narrative sculpted by someone else’s hand. A tremor started in my hands, making the edges of the paper tremble. I reread a passage about my 'defining moment,' and a wave of nausea washed over me. Who had done this? The air in the room suddenly felt thick, suffocating. I stood up abruptly, knocking my chair back with a clatter that echoed loudly. My heart hammered against my ribs, a frantic, uneven rhythm. I needed to see the handwriting, to find the seam where the truth was stitched into this fiction.

[story 2]

It happened over lukewarm coffee in the breakroom. I was reviewing some old academic papers when I stumbled upon a file labeled ‘Personal Narrative.’ My eyes scanned the text, and the blood drained from my face. The account of my early struggles, the triumphs I’d always credited to sheer grit, were presented as carefully curated anecdotes. A strange, hollow feeling settled in my chest, heavy and suffocating. I gripped the mug so tightly my knuckles turned white. I started pacing the small space, my movements jerky and agitated. I kept glancing over my shoulder, half-expecting someone to be watching, or perhaps, half-expecting the walls to shift. When I finally managed to look back at the page, the ink seemed to swim, mocking me with its polished falsehoods. I let out a shaky exhale, my whole body vibrating with a sudden, intense unease.

[story 3]

The courier had delivered the box this morning, and inside was a bound volume. When I finally opened it, the sheer volume of carefully constructed memories hit me like a physical blow. It was my life, meticulously chronicled, yet utterly alien. I sat down heavily on the floor, the hard surface digging into my legs. My vision tunneled slightly; the edges of the room seemed to blur into an indistinct haze. I touched the cover, my fingers tracing the embossed lettering, a strange, cold certainty blooming in my gut. A slow, deep shudder ran through my frame, starting in my core and spreading outward. I couldn't seem to focus on the words anymore; they were just shapes, menacing constructs. I pushed myself up, my movements slow and deliberate, as if moving through deep water, desperate to find an anchor in the sudden, terrifying disorientation.
